cards.back: A process of summarizing and visualizing datasets to understand the distribution and patterns of data.
ggplot2, base graphics, lattice, and Shiny are the main graphical packages available in R for data visualization.
A circular statistical graphic divided into slices to illustrate numerical proportion.
A graph where points are used to represent the relationship between two variables.
A graph where points on a grid are connected by straight lines to show trends and patterns.
A graphical representation of the distribution of data, showing the frequency of different values.
A graphical representation of the distribution of data, showing the median, quartiles, and outliers.
A graphical representation of categorical data, where bars are used to show the frequency or values of different categories.
Other types of graphs in R include box-whisker plots, violin plots, and heatmaps, among others.
